candidates.stmt:
I have been campaigning locally to improve my local community for several years now. I am passionate about identifying and fixing local issues and have successfully been getting things done in Almond Hill all year round. That’s why I think I could be a real asset to the local community with a voice on the council.

From addressing speeding outside Letchmore School to tackling fly tipping, damaged play equipment and overgrown areas. my aim is to support a safe local space.

I want a better community for everyone, and I want to keep working hard for my neighbours and everyone living in Almond Hill.

I've lived in Stevenage for nearly fifteen years. I've been a follower of politics in the UK and abroad since I was a teenager, but only in recent years have I been compelled to take a more active role and try to enact positive change in this country.

As a Green Party councillor I will work to make Stevenage a safe, clean, town where everyone can live, work and feel welcome. There's been a worrying shift in the tone of political debate worldwide and in the UK and I think that fighting against that needs to start at the grassroots level, not just in Parliament. I believe the Green Party offer a chance for real, positive change for the people of Stevenage which is why I'm proud to stand as a candidate.

I’m Ellie Plater and I’m proud to be re-standing as your Labour Candidate for the Bedwell County Division election on Thursday 1st May 2025.

I was honoured to be elected to this position in a by-election last August and I’m asking for your support to serve another term.

I’ve spent most of my life here. My daughter has now turned 1, and like me she’s growing up in the town I’m proud to call home. Like you, I just want the best for my family.

So far I’ve worked hard to get road repairs done, complete your casework, hold regular ward surgeries, lobby for better bus networks and push for funding for SEND provision.

But it still remains the case, that for too long, the Conservative-run County Council have let down our residents and schools and left roads, transport and social care to deteriorate. We all deserve so much better.

I want to continue to be Bedwell’s champion on the County Council, trying to fix these issues one by one. You can read what I plan to do next below!

My plan for change in our area

1. Tackle the Cost of Living Crisis - I’ll push the County Council to invest in Stevenage, boost our local economy and leave more money in your pocket.

2. Be your champion on the County Council - It’s time to move on from a failing Conservative County Council and elect Councillors who truly care about our community.

3. Introduce a SEND taskforce - We’ll set up a SEND taskforce of parents, schools and representatives to address the mess left by the Conservative County Council.

4. Crack down on anti-social behaviour - We’ll introduce a zero tolerance approach to anti-social behaviour, from enforcing fly-tipping fines to ensuring rapid police responses.

5. Finally fix Potholes - We’ll establish a road and transport improvement plan to tackle potholes and ensure we have reliable, affordable public transport.

I was born at Lister Hospital, grew up just around the corner from the Pear Tree shops, and spent my school years at Roebuck and Barnwell. From childhood weekends at Fairlands Valley Park to cheering on the Boro at Broadhall Way, this town has shaped every part of who I am.

Representing the community I love would be a profound honour.

My focus is simple: putting the people of Stevenage first, always. This means protecting our green spaces, championing the need for high-quality public housing and ensuring our council is transparent, accountable, and driven by the needs of its residents.

Stevenage is my home, and I want to make sure it remains a place we can all be proud to call our own.

As a Green Party councillor, I will work towards creating a safer and cleaner town, with accessible green spaces so that all Stevenage residents can enjoy nature, exercise and leisure activities. I will encourage more engagement with the needs of our young people, our elderly residents and disadvantaged groups. And I will advocate for further action on the climate emergency, including supporting local biodiversity and the transition to net zero.

I’ve seen discontent locally and I want to address this. I want to hear from Stevenage residents on what matters to you, so that we can work together to change our town for the better, to create a fairer local community that supports everyone.

Since you first elected me, the world has changed. The Government was unprepared for the pandemic and mishandled it. On the plus side the UK came up with a vaccine ahead of other major countries. Then the unthinkable happened: Putin unleashed a war in Europe causing massive loss of life and disruption. Today, we have a serious cost of living crisis affecting everyone, and our NHS is struggling. Luckily, in Chells we have easy access to Fairlands Valley Park and our own Chells Park, now with a new children’s playground helped with funding from me and my fellow Lib Dem councillors’ community budgets. I am therefore honoured to have served as one of your team of Lib Dem councillors, working to maintain and improve Chells. I hope you will support me for re-election on Thursday 4 May.

I joined the green party because I'm passionate about environmental conservation. I also see the Green Party as one that truly advocates for social justice and fairness.

I would be a great councillor because I have substantial background in carrying out casework on housing and welfare benefits problems having logged a total of 8 years as an advice worker.

Hello, I am Maureen McKay and I am proud to have been chosen to stand as your Labour & Co-operative candidate for Pin Green.  I have lived in Pin Green for over 50 years and really feel part of this community. Originally from Scotland and the eldest of four, I married at 19 and moved to London with my husband Gerard and I worked for IBM. Unemployment rates were high in Port Glasgow where we grew up.  We moved to Pin Green, Stevenage in 1964 as part of the London overspill and I had 3 daughters all born at home. The Lister Hospital wasn’t built then but all the doctors were obstetricians and we had great midwifery services. 

Stevenage offered great facilities for families: good schools, local churches, play schemes, adventure playgrounds, Bowes Lyon etc. The large companies we worked for (BAE and Kodak) both offered great social facilities for employees and their families. 

I retrained to be a teacher in 1974 and taught Maths at The John Henry Newman school in Stevenage for 25 years. I was the NUT (now NEU) representative and took part in many demonstrations in London fighting for an increase in teachers’ pay.  I really enjoyed my years of teaching. Even now after years of retirement I constantly come across ex pupils and it is always a pleasure to chat to them about what has happened since they left school. 

In my retirement, I have enjoyed being a local councillor for nearly 9 years. I am very grateful to the Stevenage Borough Council and our wonderful community for the support that we as a family have enjoyed over the years. I now feel that I can continue help to pay some of this back by supporting and helping others improve their quality of life in our town during this most difficult time. 

I am a Labour and Cooperative councillor, and a member of the Labour Women’ s Forum which supports the Co-operative principles of equality and collaborative work. I look forward to supporting all residents, small businesses and schools/playgroups and nurseries in t

I'm Thea Pendlebury, your Labour Candidate for the Roebuck Stevenage Borough Council by-election. I’m sending you this letter because I would like to persuade you to vote for me in the Roebuck by-election on Thursday, 30th October.

In Roebuck, you have Labour councillors who are doing great work within the ward. Whether it’s getting the lighting and windows upgraded at the Crossroads Community Hub, working with the police to tackle anti-social behaviour at Shephalbury park, or leading litter picking sessions in the ward, your councillors have been working hard for you. However, there is more to be done. I will work closely with our MP Kevin Bonavia to keep delivering for Roebuck.

Reform councils have been focusing their efforts on pushing ideological policies. They should be working on improving local services and making their communities stronger. As your Labour candidate, I will work hard to improve everyday life for residents.

Thanks to Labour’s investment in Roebuck Surgery, local patients will be getting better access to appointments and improved services. At the same time Reform continues to talk about replacing our NHS with insurance companies competing against each other, putting profits before patients.

Stevenage is my home, and home to many of the people I love most in the world. I was born and raised here, and have been shaped by the grit, determination and compassion of our diverse community.

I've also seen so many who are already struggling be forced, again and again, to bear the brunt of funding cuts and questionable policy decisions, while their concerns are dismissed or ignored, in a system that is no longer fit for purpose. I joined the Green Party to challenge this failing status quo with solutions that focus on the voices and needs of working class people; Stevenage was once ahead of its time in this regard, and I believe it will be again if we work together.

In the decade I have lived in the Old Town, I have fallen in love with its unique character, and I am deeply passionate about the people and places that make our community such a vibrant and special place to live. I believe that by working closely with residents and local businesses, we can make make the local environment a better place to live.

I have a master's degree in development studies and have worked for charity and campaign groups for almost all of my life. I currently work as a consultant on environment and social issues, but as I move towards retirement I want to shift my time and energy towards my own local community.

It’s been a pleasure to meet many of you on the doorstep and hear from you about what matters to you in your area. Many of you have told me that life is harder for you and you feel let down by people and institutions that should make things better. People tell me they feel neglected by unresponsive Conservative councillors. Compare this with what Liberal Democrats do for people elsewhere in Hertfordshire. I am prepared to work tirelessly for a better deal for children, for decent roads, for better public transport and for more environment friendly measures. Please give me your support by voting for positive change and a county councillor that's on your side. Thank you.

View Alistair's video here:  https://youtu.be/MXnQu6Xm_nM

View Alistair's election address here:  https://1drv.ms/b/s!Atog3CZwpXPCgbtLIE2PIAgAXfdJ0A?e=Ri0XeS

A message from Alistair: I am a passionate believer that everyone contributes to making Stevenage a fantastic town to live in and I want to help Longmeadow residents make a difference in their neighbourhood.  I want to work with community groups that have ideas they want to put into action.

I love living in Stevenage because it is so green – nobody is more than a few hundred metres from a green space, whether it is a park, playing field, woodland or countryside.  Our Labour-led council has shown its commitment to maintaining our parks, creating wild areas to support wildlife, and investing in community orchards. I’m particularly interested in looking at ways to balance a green environment with the need for housing. I also want people to feel safe in their neighbourhood and have pride in it, and will do everything I can to be tough on antisocial behaviour and fly-tipping.

Professionally, I have twenty years’ experience in the pharmaceutical industry. My voluntary work has included working with offenders at Stevenage Probation Service and with people with severe physical and learning disabilities. I have also previously served as a director of the company managing the block of flats in which I lived.

As someone who was born disabled, I understand the difficulties faced by disabled people in accessing services.  But more than this, it has given me a sensitivity to how people and groups can be marginalised and socially excluded.  I will work with community groups to ensure their voices are heard.

Having moved to Stevenage a few years ago, I have been struck by the friendliness and welcoming nature of the local community. Not only that, but Stevenage offers a truly wonderful place to live, with beautiful green spaces and cycle pathways promoting a healthy lifestyle.

I strongly believe that the current political system is broken. The people of Stevenage are hard working and proud, but too many re now struggling to get by due to a failure of previous governments. I’d like to advocate for my local community and ensure the issues that really matter to you are heard by those in power.

I wish to offer the voters of Harlow a different choice from the tired old parties. My main concerns are the poor state of our NHS, sewage in our waters, the cost of living crisis and profiteering of the big banks, energy and water companies. We need to have positive practical solutions to the daily lives of people, not the gimmicky, punch-and-judy politics of late. I believe the Liberal Democrats can offer those solutions, providing families with some relief from their daily expenses, the elderly and infirm dignity in their care and the young with hope to look forward with.

The two big parties will say that a vote for Liberal Democrats is a wasted vote – but I believe that our positive policies compare incredibly well with theirs - and a vote for me will send them a message that they cannot take you and your vote for granted. I will be a strong voice and MP for you, championing your causes and ensuring things get done here. 

If you have always voted for other parties, I would ask you to be bold, embrace change, and give me your vote so you can get a fresh, new MP who will work hard and work diligently for you, your family and your community.

I’m Claire Parris and I’m proud to be standing as your Labour Candidate for the St Nicholas County Council Division election on Thursday 1st May.

I was born and raised in Stevenage and I take pride in calling St Nicholas my home.

The current Conservative County Councillor has been unseen and unbothered.

He’s let down my area. My home.

But I know what real public service looks like.

I work for our NHS, at the Lister, and I’ve served our community as a Borough Councillor for the past 6 years.

For too long, the Conservative-run County Council have let down our residents and schools and left roads, transport and social care to deteriorate. We all deserve so much better. It’s time to Heart Herts!

I want to be your champion on the County Council, fixing these issues one by one. It’s time to vote for change. And you can read my plan for change below!

My plan for change in our area

1. Tackle the Cost of Living Crisis - I’ll push the County Council to invest in Stevenage, boost our local economy and leave more money in your pocket.

2. Be your champion on the County Council - It’s time to move on from unseen, unbothered Conservative County Councillors and elect a Councillor who cares about our community.

3. Introduce a SEND taskforce - We’ll set up a SEND taskforce of parents, schools and representatives to address the mess left by the Conservative County Council.

4. Crack down on anti-social behaviour - We’ll introduce a zero tolerance approach to anti-social behaviour, from enforcing fly-tipping fines to ensuring rapid police responses.

5. Finally fix Potholes - We’ll establish a road and transport improvement plan to tackle potholes and ensure we have reliable, affordable public transport.

I dream of a future where families, children, elderly & loved ones across Stevenage are given the opportunity to thrive. I'm tired of watching our town be torn apart by divisive politics and want to bring the focus back to serving the people in your community.

Many of us right now probably feel like we aren't being heard, let alone understood. Brexit, COVID and almost a decade and a half of austerity are all still having rippling effects across all communities. We face many challenges but to overcome them, we have to start by listening to those who have the most at stake. For me, that means representing those in St. Nicholas so we can build a fairer future together.

I am standing for the Green Party because I believe that the Greens have the best plan for the economy as well as the environment. I favour a holistic approach in politics at the local as well as the national level.

As a keen allotment grower and having many years experience as a professional gardener, I am very interested in encouraging the promotion of home grown vegetables as a way of reducing the food bill and helping the environment. A personal growth and fitness enthusiast, I have been teaching Tae Kwon Do for over 50 years and I hope to promote fitness and relaxation as well as environmental improvements in our area.

I believe our community deserves better than being taken for granted. For too long, decisions have been made without any challenge. When one party dominates for decades, accountability slips and local people are left dealing with the consequences.

I work for a local charity, supporting people through some of the most difficult moments in their lives, and that experience has shown me how deeply issues like housing, mental health and underfunded services affect our community, and how often people feel ignored.

I grew up in Symonds Green, still live here now, and I want to listen, challenge poor decisions, and stand up for this area in any way I can.

I’ve lived in Old Stevenage with my family for more than 25 years. I’m a businesswoman and I know it’s my time to give back. I can’t just leave it to someone else to take the reins, it would be passing the buck. I am keen to step in and deliver improvements for the residents of Old Town and Symonds Green.

I know I’ve got big shoes to fill. Cllr Graham Lawrence, who is retiring as Old Stevenage’s County Councillor, did a lot to improve the Old Town and Symonds Green. He also ensured that any changes to the High Street were done using common sense and for the benefit of both businesses and residents. I want to build on these successes and make our High Street more inviting.

Somewhere people want to spend a little time either getting their hair cut; some flowers for their beautiful wife or mother; and then in the evening, wining and dining with friends after a hard day’s work.

County Council has a plan to rejuvenate the High Street; to make it a little safer; a place people will want to buy a coffee or have their car repaired.

We, the Conservatives in Hertfordshire County Council, want to do all this but we can’t do so without your vote.

I have lived in Stevenage since 1956 and have grown up in the town, attending Longmeadow Infant and Junior Schools and Barnwell when it was a Secondary Modern School. 

I married in 1975, had two children, a girl and a boy, who attended local Schools and then when onto Universities. 

I have previously been a Stevenage Borough Councillor for a total of 28 years, serving on many committees, which has given me a wealth of knowledge and experience within Local Government, which I can take forward at County level. 

During my time as a Borough Councillor I also had the honour of being The Mayor twice-2018-2019 and 2022-2023. Carrying out many Civic duties and engaging with the many wonderful organisations throughout our town. I have also for many years been a member of some of these organisations, The Stevenage, Ingelheim, Autun Twinning Association. The Stevenage Kadoma Link and a local Community Association. I have recently become a member of a Primary Care Patient Participation Group and a Trustee of the Stevenage District Scouts. 

I have in the past been a School Governor, serving for over forty years. 

I enjoy spending my free time with family and friends and supporting the Performing Arts.

I first moved to Stevenage with my parents back in 1967 and have been in love with our town ever since.

Growing up in the town, I became aware of the great sense of togetherness and community we had and it's saddening to now see people working actively to take that away from us.

I initially became involved in local politics in the 1970s and have been an active member of the local Green Party since 2015. As Woodfield's first Green councillor I will be there to listen to you and represent your views, especially regarding the social and environmental issues currently prominent in the area.

I grew up in Hertfordshire and I moved to Stevenage a few years ago, looking for a quiet spot to live with my two rescue cats Indy and Nala.

I am a lawyer working in criminal justice and I prosecute and defend in some of the most serious cases, including as a specialist in organised crime and rape and serious sexual offences. I volunteer to support those from disadvantaged backgrounds in gaining entry to the law.

If I was elected as your councillor I would advocate for your needs and improving your local area and access to essential services.
